reports.title: UK Electricity Mix Weekly Report: Friday 19 June
reports.intro: A measured weekly report for EnergyMix.info using the available 7-day historical generation feed for Saturday 13 June to Thursday 18 June.
reports.summary: The week from Saturday 13 June to Thursday 18 June averaged about 23.0 GW of measured generation, with renewables averaging 46.0% of measured output. The strongest renewable day in the available data was Saturday 13 June, when wind, solar and hydro together averaged 68.7% of measured generation. Gas was highest on Tuesday 16 June and lowest on Saturday 13 June.
reports.takeaway: For flexible electricity use, the cleanest opportunities are likely to appear when wind is strong, gas is low and demand is not at a peak. This is the foundation for future EV-charging guidance, clean-electricity alerts and weekly newsletter summaries.
drivers: Renewables were strongest on Saturday 13 June, when wind, solar and hydro averaged 68.7% of measured generation.
Wind was the largest swing factor in the available feed, peaking at about 11.5 GW average output on Saturday 13 June.
Solar was strongest on Saturday 13 June, averaging about 4.5 GW across settlement periods after embedded-solar backfill where needed.
Gas averaged about 7.3 GW across the period, rising highest on Tuesday 16 June and falling lowest on Saturday 13 June.
Measured generation was highest on Thursday 18 June, at roughly 25.0 GW average output.

cleanestPeriods.title: Best low-carbon proxy: Saturday 13 June
cleanestPeriods.body: Historical carbon-intensity aggregates are not yet part of this weekly report feed, so EnergyMix.info uses renewable share and gas output as cautious proxies. Saturday 13 June had the strongest renewable share at 68.7%.

cleanestPeriods.title: Lowest gas proxy: Saturday 13 June
cleanestPeriods.body: Gas generation averaged about 2.9 GW on Saturday 13 June. Lower gas periods are often cleaner, but the report does not yet claim this was the lowest-carbon period without historical carbon-intensity validation.

higherCarbonPeriods.title: Highest gas proxy: Tuesday 16 June
higherCarbonPeriods.body: Gas averaged about 10.9 GW on Tuesday 16 June. Gas-heavy periods are usually higher-carbon, but this is labelled as a proxy until historical carbon-intensity highs/lows are added to the report feed.

highlights.label: Wind
highlights.value: Highest average wind output was about 11.5 GW on Saturday 13 June.

highlights.label: Solar
highlights.value: Highest average solar output was about 4.5 GW on Saturday 13 June.

highlights.label: Gas
highlights.value: Highest average gas output was about 10.9 GW on Tuesday 16 June; lowest was about 2.9 GW on Saturday 13 June.

reports.methodologyNote: This report is generated from the available 7-day historical generation feed and validated against external public data. Carbon-intensity highs/lows and interconnector summaries should be treated as future additions unless they are present as validated historical aggregates.

yesterday.title: UK Electricity Mix Yesterday: Thursday 18 June
yesterday.summary: Yesterday, Thursday 18 June, measured average generation was about 25.0 GW. Renewables averaged 39.9% of measured generation, gas averaged 9.7 GW, wind averaged 6.1 GW and solar averaged 3.4 GW.

highestRenewableShare.text: Current measured 7-day high: 68.7% average renewable share on Saturday 13 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestWindGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 11.5 GW average wind output on Saturday 13 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestSolarGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 4.5 GW average solar output on Saturday 13 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestGasGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 10.9 GW average gas output on Tuesday 16 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

socialPosts.title: Monday weekly grid brief
socialPosts.body:
Last week in Britain’s electricity mix:

• Highest renewable share in the available feed: 68.7% on Saturday 13 June
• Highest average wind output: ~11.5 GW on Saturday 13 June
• Highest average solar output: ~4.5 GW on Saturday 13 June
• Highest average gas output: ~10.9 GW on Tuesday 16 June

The shift was clear: gas-heavy weekdays gave way to a cleaner, wind-led weekend.

Full report: https://energymix.info/reports/weekly/2026-06-19/

socialPosts.title: Wednesday explainer
socialPosts.body:
What does “UK electricity mix” actually mean?

For live grid dashboards, it usually means Great Britain’s electricity system: England, Scotland and Wales. Northern Ireland is part of the UK, but operates in a separate electricity market.

Explainer: https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-mix

socialPosts.title: Friday practical clean-electricity post
socialPosts.body:
The cleanest time to use electricity is not a fixed hour.

It changes with wind, solar, demand, imports and gas generation. Windy overnight periods and sunny middays can be much cleaner than still early-evening peaks.

Guide: https://energymix.info/cleanest-time-to-use-electricity
